question: Can you describe a time when you designed and deployed a scalable ML system from scratch? Discuss the architecture, challenges faced, and how you ensured scalability and fault tolerance.
expected_key_points: Detailed architecture of the ML system (e.g., microservices, batch/real-time pipelines)
Scalability considerations (e.g., load balancing, distributed training, model serving)
Fault tolerance mechanisms (e.g., retries, circuit breakers, monitoring)
Impact metrics (e.g., latency reduction, throughput improvement)

question: Explain how you would design a real-time agentic system for automated lead triaging using LangChain or Crew AI. What components would you include, and how would you ensure low latency and high accuracy?
expected_key_points: Agentic workflow design (e.g., tool integration, memory, decision-making)
Real-time processing strategies (e.g., streaming, caching, async I/O)
Accuracy optimization (e.g., prompt engineering, model ensemble, feedback loops)
Latency mitigation (e.g., model quantization, edge deployment)

question: How would you implement a forecasting system for seller demand using time-series data? Discuss your approach to feature engineering, model selection, and evaluation.
expected_key_points: Feature engineering (e.g., lag features, rolling statistics, external data integration)
Model selection (e.g., ARIMA, Prophet, LSTM, Transformer-based models)
Evaluation metrics (e.g., MAE, RMSE, MAPE, business impact)
Deployment strategy (e.g., batch vs. real-time, A/B testing)

question: Describe your experience with distributed training or inference for ML models. How would you optimize a PyTorch model for multi-GPU training or low-latency inference in production?
expected_key_points: Distributed training frameworks (e.g., PyTorch Distributed, Horovod)
Optimization techniques (e.g., mixed precision, gradient checkpointing, sharding)
Inference optimization (e.g., ONNX, TensorRT, model pruning)
Challenges and trade-offs (e.g., data parallelism vs. model parallelism)

question: Your resume highlights experience with LangChain and agentic systems, but the JD emphasizes RL and forecasting systems. Can you discuss any experience you have with reinforcement learning, or describe how you would approach designing an RL-based system for seller demand forecasting?
expected_key_points: Understanding of RL fundamentals (e.g., Markov Decision Processes, Q-learning, PPO)
Application to forecasting (e.g., dynamic pricing, inventory optimization)
Challenges in RL (e.g., exploration vs. exploitation, reward shaping)
Comparison with traditional forecasting methods

question: Tell me about a time when you had to deal with ambiguous requirements for an ML project. How did you approach the problem, and what steps did you take to distill the requirements into a clear ML problem statement?
expected_key_points: Approach to ambiguity (e.g., stakeholder interviews, prototyping, iterative refinement)
Problem definition (e.g., framing as classification/regression, defining success metrics)
Communication strategies (e.g., translating business needs to technical specs)
Outcome and lessons learned

question: Imagine you are leading a team to build a new GenAI system for Amazon sellers, but your initial model performs poorly in production due to distribution shift. How would you diagnose the issue, and what steps would you take to improve the system?
expected_key_points: Diagnosis (e.g., data drift analysis, model monitoring, A/B testing)
Root cause analysis (e.g., feature mismatch, label noise, concept drift)
Remediation (e.g., retraining, model updates, fallback strategies)
Preventive measures (e.g., continuous monitoring, CI/CD for models)
